content:
Audio Dataset Manager is a comprehensive toolkit designed to streamline the preparation of audio datasets for TTS (Text-to-Speech) training and voice cloning projects.
Born from the need to efficiently process dozens of hours of audiobook data for voice cloning, this tool bridges the gap between raw audio files and training-ready datasets.
Features:
Audio analysis and silence detection Format conversion (MP3/WAV) Intelligent splitting into training-ready clips (0.6–11s) Auto-transcription powered by OpenAI Whisper Interactive dataset review UI JSON management with automatic backup and versioning View on GitHub
summary:
Audio Dataset Manager is a comprehensive toolkit designed to streamline the preparation of audio datasets for TTS (Text-to-Speech) training and voice cloning projects.

Born from the need to efficiently process dozens of hours of audiobook data for voice cloning, this tool bridges the gap between raw audio files and training-ready datasets.
title: Audio Dataset Manager

content:
Harmonics is a contemplative game made in 48h for Global Game Jam 2018.
You play as a pair of forest spirits that can explore various places on a magical floating island, and awaken flowers by singing to them. Flowers may awaken other flowers in turn, and cause chain reactions.
Ideally the game is co-op: one player controls the movement with a joystick, and one controls the singing by playing bass at a microphone.
![](/img/projects/harmonics/JB6H2V.png "Harmonics — green world") ![](/img/projects/harmonics/Kp2XwW.png "Harmonics") ![](/img/projects/harmonics/XYG7bx.png "Harmonics") ![](/img/projects/harmonics/zMVFa4.png "Harmonics") ![](/img/projects/harmonics/zTVLpB.png "Harmonics") View on itch.io
summary:
Harmonics is a contemplative game made in 48h for Global Game Jam 2018.

You play as a pair of forest spirits that can explore various places on a magical floating island, and awaken flowers by singing to them. Flowers may awaken other flowers in turn, and cause chain reactions.
title: Harmonics

content:
Traces is a project made in the framework of my PhD thesis about XR technologies, art and Cultural Heritage. It’s a contemplative experience where you find yourself in a ruined environment, where you have only fragments and hidden elements that allow you to restitute the story of the beings who lived there.
Through this project, I wanted to express why I’m so attached and moved by Cultural Heritage: the tension between disappearance and survival, resistance to death.
Made with Charlotte, who made the FX.
Presented at: Colloque-exposition Valeurs de l’Empreinte, Université Lumière Lyon II, March 2023.
![](/img/projects/traces/lyXBvW.jpg "Traces — desert landscape") ![](/img/projects/traces/HiWZWY.jpg "Traces — ruined room") ![](/img/projects/traces/cpxEiG.jpg "Traces — stone figures") ![](/img/projects/traces/je8lSz.jpg "Traces") ![](/img/projects/traces/vNwjDZ.jpg "Traces") View on itch.io
summary: Traces is a project made in the framework of my PhD thesis about XR technologies, art and Cultural Heritage. It’s a contemplative experience where you find yourself in a ruined environment, where you have only fragments and hidden elements that allow you to restitute the story of the beings who lived there.
title: Traces

content:
This is a game about being nice and cosy at home.
The player can do several different activities: change their clothing (well, their onesie), play a horror video game, browse the web, drink some hot tea, or read a whole book. Or maybe not the whole thing — there’s a bookmark system to pick up where you left off.
Made with Charlotte and Camille Couturier for Global Game Jam 2019. 3D assets made with Blender, audio samples from Freesounds.org, retro game effects made with bfxr, public domain literature from the Gutenberg project. Made with Unity.
![](/img/projects/cozy/fjMOHk.png "Cosy — living room") ![](/img/projects/cozy/G7wXue.png "Cosy") ![](/img/projects/cozy/iwLYuh.png "Cosy") ![](/img/projects/cozy/l-Xclk.png "Cosy") ![](/img/projects/cozy/Quj7LM.png "Cosy") ![](/img/projects/cozy/XSGFY5.png "Cosy") ![](/img/projects/cozy/yMQH85.png "Cosy") View on itch.io
summary:

title: Cosy

content:
Diffusers in ComfyUI is a custom node that integrates the Hugging Face Diffusers pipeline directly into ComfyUI. Available through ComfyUI Manager and the Comfy Registry.
Supports txt2img, img2img, inpainting, LoRA, and BLoRA workflows across SD 1.5 and SDXL models.
![](/img/projects/diffusers_comfyui_screenshot.png "Diffusers in ComfyUI — txt2img workflow") View on GitHub
summary:
Diffusers in ComfyUI is a custom node that integrates the Hugging Face Diffusers pipeline directly into ComfyUI. Available through ComfyUI Manager and the Comfy Registry.

Supports txt2img, img2img, inpainting, LoRA, and BLoRA workflows across SD 1.5 and SDXL models.
title: Diffusers in ComfyUI

content:
Mon Petit Atelier de Destruction du Patrimoine (“My Little Heritage Destruction Workshop”), or PADP for short, is a VR game where you get to visit a virtual museum. You can scan the objects with a tablet, to acquire knowledge about them, grab them to observe them more closely… and smash them.
This project explores the concept of sacredness and cultural heritage. What does it feel like to go against these rules we were taught our whole lives?
Requirements: VR headset (tested with HTC Vive and Vive Pro, SteamVR). Minimal play area of 2.5m × 2m.
View on itch.io
summary: Mon Petit Atelier de Destruction du Patrimoine (“My Little Heritage Destruction Workshop”), or PADP for short, is a VR game where you get to visit a virtual museum. You can scan the objects with a tablet, to acquire knowledge about them, grab them to observe them more closely… and smash them.
title: Mon Petit Atelier de Destruction du Patrimoine

content:
Kintsukuroi is the Japanese art of repairing broken pottery by mending it with gold, to display the object’s history rather than hiding it by fixing it back to its original state.
A spirit has hatched, but this ordeal put it in a bad state. Explore a beautiful and vibrant world, and feed on anything you can find to grow and heal.
Made for Global Game Jam 2020: Repair. Programming and character graphics by Charlotte. Environment by Maëlys Jusseaux and Camille Couturier. Music by Chad Crouch on Free Music Archive.
![](/img/projects/kintsukuroi/VcPwHa.jpg "Kintsukuroi — character art") ![](/img/projects/kintsukuroi/8AUPo1.jpg "Kintsukuroi — green world") ![](/img/projects/kintsukuroi/nciiNa.jpg "Kintsukuroi") View on itch.io
summary: Kintsukuroi is the Japanese art of repairing broken pottery by mending it with gold, to display the object’s history rather than hiding it by fixing it back to its original state.
title: Kintsukuroi

content:
Since October 2019, the extraordinary fame of Mona Lisa has pushed the Musée du Louvre to implement additional safety measures — visitors now have to queue through barriers to see the painting.
The Louvre also offers an official VR experience allowing visitors to meet face to face with Lisa Gherardini, removing the crowd and the difficulty of access. Although laudable, we can wonder: what if the current conditions of visiting Mona Lisa, however unpleasant they are, were actually part of the authentic experience in the 21st century?
Through this experience, we deliberately chose to replicate the exact conditions of visiting Mona Lisa today, as a humorous, playful and imaginative way of reflecting about core concepts of cultural heritage: originality and authenticity.
Any pictures or selfies you take are saved in the “Pics” folder next to the game’s executable.
Made with Charlotte.
![](/img/projects/mona/IY1PY1.png "Mona VR") ![](/img/projects/mona/SmfTft.png "Mona VR") ![](/img/projects/mona/VcjAt0.png "Mona VR") ![](/img/projects/mona/Wm447N.png "Mona VR") Requirements: VR headset (tested with HTC Vive and Vive Pro, SteamVR).
Presented at: ConVRgence (VRIC) Virtual Reality International Conference, Laval, April 2020.
View on itch.io
summary: Since October 2019, the extraordinary fame of Mona Lisa has pushed the Musée du Louvre to implement additional safety measures — visitors now have to queue through barriers to see the painting.
title: Mona VR

content:
This project was made in the framework of a PhD thesis. It aimed at exploring my own imaginary projected upon the Middle Ages. It’s a contemplative experience where you can sit next to Eleanor of Aquitaine, as though she was reading you a story while her minstrels are gently playing music.
Made with Charlotte, who made all the characters and animations, the central scene in general, and all the technical art (FX and shaders).
![](/img/projects/alienor/fMyHGJ.jpg "Alienor — Eleanor of Aquitaine") ![](/img/projects/alienor/MTIFaj.jpg "Alienor — medieval scene") ![](/img/projects/alienor/S_GvXW.jpg "Alienor") ![](/img/projects/alienor/CdAReF.png "Alienor — dark forest") ![](/img/projects/alienor/egdq3l.png "Alienor") ![](/img/projects/alienor/rXwA1t.png "Alienor") View on itch.io
summary: This project was made in the framework of a PhD thesis. It aimed at exploring my own imaginary projected upon the Middle Ages. It’s a contemplative experience where you can sit next to Eleanor of Aquitaine, as though she was reading you a story while her minstrels are gently playing music.
title: Alienor

content:
Help lost souls to find themselves, by aiding them in their personal struggles.
Talk to the ghost. Pick up ingredients. Make your potion in the cauldron. Give the ghost its potion. Repeat. There are 5 ghosts to help. Made in 48h with Charlotte RGB for Global Game Jam 2021.
Tags: Atmospheric · Casual · Emotional · Exploration · Fantasy · Female Protagonist · Relaxing · Short
Controls: Move — WASD / Arrow Keys / Left Joystick · Action — Spacebar / controller face button
![](/img/projects/eira/RzKrcQ.png "Eira's Potions — lakeside") ![](/img/projects/eira/7bXFL6.png "Eira's Potions — witch") ![](/img/projects/eira/SgWq0a.png "Eira's Potions") ![](/img/projects/eira/qVzZ0a.png "Eira's Potions") ![](/img/projects/eira/uOx-p5.png "Eira's Potions") ![](/img/projects/eira/wbE9vk.png "Eira's Potions") <iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/bgyFL90jCj8" title="Eira's Potions for the Lost — video" allowfullscreen></iframe> View on itch.io
summary:
Help lost souls to find themselves, by aiding them in their personal struggles.

  1. Talk to the ghost.
  2. Pick up ingredients.
  3. Make your potion in the cauldron.
  4. Give the ghost its potion.
  5. Repeat. There are 5 ghosts to help.

Made in 48h with Charlotte RGB for Global Game Jam 2021.
title: Eira's Potions for the Lost

content:
I created this cathedral in the framework of a master’s and later PhD research about augmented and virtual reality applied to Cultural Heritage. This VR application, built in Unity, lets you navigate the different architectural polychromy schemes of a Gothic cathedral — focusing on the 13th and 19th centuries. I worked with art historian Anne Vuillemard-Jenn to restitute as plausible decorative schemes as possible. Alongside the historical reconstructions, I also created “emotional” décors that try to convey the different receptions and reinterpretations of medieval architecture across both periods.

summary:
Resilience was created with Fabienne Bouvier and Chloé Leroux as a school project, and was exhibited at the Centre d’Arts d’Enghien-les-Bains.

The project takes the form of a virtual exhibition of a fictive artist. Each artwork can be interacted with to reveal traces and echoes of the artist’s life — fragments of memory, emotion, and identity embedded in the work itself.
title: Resilience

summary:
Dreamcatcher was made with Juliette Schmidt and Thi Nguyen as a school project.

It is a VR application built in Unreal Engine. The player must flee through several shifting environments. In each one, they must find a mask and physically put it on — literally — in order to escape the entity relentlessly chasing them.
title: Dreamcatcher

content:
Merveilles de l’Atelier d’André Breton is a Unity application made in collaboration with the Association André Breton.
A selection of objects from André Breton’s personal studio were 3D scanned, optimized and integrated into an interactive Unity application. The application is currently exhibited at the Centre Pompidou in Paris. The 3D models are also available on my Sketchfab account.
![](/img/projects/andrebreton/Screenshot_from_2026-03-14_00-21-44.png "Merveilles — object view") ![](/img/projects/andrebreton/Screenshot_from_2026-03-14_00-21-53.png "Merveilles — object view") ![](/img/projects/andrebreton/Screenshot_from_2026-03-14_00-22-10.png "Merveilles — object view") ![](/img/projects/andrebreton/Screenshot_from_2026-03-14_00-22-29.png "Merveilles — object view") ![](/img/projects/andrebreton/Screenshot_from_2026-03-14_00-22-39.png "Merveilles — object view") ![](/img/projects/andrebreton/Screenshot_from_2026-03-14_00-23-40.png "Merveilles — object view") ![](/img/projects/andrebreton/Screenshot_from_2026-03-14_00-25-00.png "Merveilles — object view") ![](/img/projects/andrebreton/Screenshot_from_2026-03-14_00-25-07.png "Merveilles — object view") ![](/img/projects/andrebreton/Screenshot_from_2026-03-14_00-25-15.png "Merveilles — object view")
summary:
Merveilles de l’Atelier d’André Breton is a Unity application made in collaboration with the Association André Breton.

A selection of objects from André Breton’s personal studio were 3D scanned, optimized and integrated into an interactive Unity application. The application is currently exhibited at the Centre Pompidou in Paris. The 3D models are also available on my Sketchfab account.
title: Merveilles de l'Atelier d'André Breton

content:
R&D Engineer with 10 years of experience at the intersection of 3D, XR technologies and Artificial Intelligence. Currently at Nfinite, I design and deploy large-scale production pipelines combining generative AI, computer vision and 3D.
My journey began with a PhD on eliciting transcendence experiences with cultural heritage through immersive technologies — what I called the numinous heritage experience. This research taught me to think of technology not as an end in itself, but as a vector for emotion and human impact.
Today I build tools in Generative AI, Computer Vision and audio AI, always asking the same question: how can this technology concretely improve people’s lives?
I’m looking to join a team where technology has meaning — from healthcare to creativity, accessibility and beyond.
summary: R&D Engineer with 10 years of experience at the intersection of 3D, XR technologies and Artificial Intelligence. Currently at Nfinite, I design and deploy large-scale production pipelines combining generative AI, computer vision and 3D.
title: About
